commit.message:
feat: intelligent reminder lead time based on task type

- Meeting/appointment: remind 30 min before (not at start time)
- Payment/bills: remind 3 days before
- Deadline/submission: remind 1 day before
- Travel/flight: remind 2 hours before
- Medical appointment: remind 1 hour before
- Other timed tasks: remind 15 min before
- No time specified: remind at 9am on due date

Also: reminder confirmation always shows actual remind time vs due time

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: implement Priority 2 — MEDIUM IMPACT features (#23)

* feat: implement Priority 2 — MEDIUM IMPACT features

Date / Time Functions (src/translator/functions.rs + src/connection.rs):
- EXTRACT(part FROM expr) → CAST(STRFTIME(fmt, expr) AS INTEGER)
- DATE_FROM_PARTS(y, m, d) → DATE(PRINTF('%04d-%02d-%02d', ...))
- TIME_FROM_PARTS(h, m, s) → TIME(PRINTF('%02d:%02d:%02d', ...))
- TIMESTAMP_FROM_PARTS(y, m, d, hh, mm, ss) → DATETIME(PRINTF(...))
- LAST_DAY(date) → DATE(date, 'start of month', '+1 month', '-1 day')
- TO_DATE / TO_CHAR: replaced simple rules with custom SQLite functions
  that handle the optional format-string second argument
- NEXT_DAY(date, dayname): custom function using Julian Day arithmetic
- CONVERT_TIMEZONE: custom passthrough function (SQLite limitation)

Semi-Structured Functions (src/connection.rs):
- ARRAY_SLICE, ARRAY_APPEND, ARRAY_CONCAT, ARRAY_COMPACT, ARRAY_UNIQUE
- OBJECT_KEYS, STRIP_NULL_VALUE
- TYPEOF → snowflake_typeof() custom function (avoids conflict with SQLite
  built-in typeof() which uses storage-class names, not Snowflake type names)

DDL Constructs:
- CREATE TRANSIENT TABLE → strips TRANSIENT keyword (simple rule)
- CREATE TEMPORARY TABLE → passes through to SQLite natively
- CREATE DATABASE / DROP DATABASE → added to noop list
- ANALYZE → added to noop list
- MERGE INTO → added to noop list (SQLite fundamental limitation)
- ALTER TABLE RENAME COLUMN → passes through + integration test

Operators:
- :: cast operator → CAST(expr AS TYPE) via regex rewrite

Bug fix (src/translator/types.rs):
- Add trailing \b to TIME and TIMESTAMP type patterns to prevent them
  from incorrectly matching TIME_FROM_PARTS / TIMESTAMP_FROM_PARTS

TODO.md: mark GREATEST/LEAST and all Priority 2 items as complete

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: resolve clippy warnings; add lint step to new-change command

- Replace needless splitn with split() in apply_strftime (connection.rs)
- Replace manual split_once pattern with split_once() idiom
- Add 'cargo clippy' as Step 5 in new-change.md before pushing,
  and add clippy check to PR test plan checklist

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add http/https native modules and fix Fastify codegen for string methods

- New http.rs stdlib: client-side http/https using reqwest (request, get,
  ClientRequest write/end/on, IncomingMessage statusCode/on)
- Register http/https in NATIVE_MODULES, wire up codegen dispatch + FFI decls
- Fix Fastify return types: html/text/redirect/send return f64 (undefined),
  not i64 handle — was causing verifier errors
- Fix Fastify string args: use bitcast instead of ensure_i64 to preserve
  NaN-box tags needed by string_from_nanboxed in runtime
- Fix Fastify string return methods: param/method/url/header return string
  pointers, NaN-box with STRING_TAG not POINTER_TAG
- Process http pending events in async bridge poll loop

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix recursive Doxygen API Docs link in navigation

Changed "API Docs" link from relative path (doxygen/html/index.html) to
absolute path (/doxygen/html/index.html) to prevent infinite redirect loop.

Previously, when clicking "API Docs" from within the Doxygen pages, the
relative path would append to the current URL:
  /doxygen/html/index.html → /doxygen/html/doxygen/html/index.html
  → /doxygen/html/doxygen/html/doxygen/html/index.html (infinite)

Now uses absolute path from site root, ensuring the link always goes to
the correct location regardless of where you click it from.

commit.message:
refactor: treat api-token connector secrets as user secrets (#4156)

Api-token connector "connected" status is now derived state — if the user
has all required secrets stored as user secrets, the connector is connected.
This eliminates the semantic split between `secret set` and `connector connect`
for api-token types. OAuth connectors remain unchanged.

- Add getApiTokenRequiredSecretNames and deriveApiTokenConnectedTypes helpers
- Make connectorResponseSchema.id nullable for derived connectors
- Delete /api/connectors/:type/token endpoint and connectorTokenContract
- Delete upsertApiTokenConnector from connector-service
- Update listConnectors/getConnector to derive api-token status from user secrets
- Update deleteConnector to handle derived api-token connectors
- Remove api-token special-casing from build-context credential resolution
- CLI connector connect now uses PUT /api/secrets instead of token endpoint
- CLI secret list annotates user secrets matching connector patterns

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add per-chat conversation history (last 10 turns)

- PhotoStore.add_to_history / get_history, capped at _HISTORY_LIMIT=10
- call_llm accepts history and injects entries as plain-text turns
  between the system prompt and the current multimodal user message

  photo-only intakes are also logged so the LLM knows a bill was shared

  "[new photo] <recovered_pending>", "[voice message]", or plain text
- Tests updated: exact-arg assertions → call_args kwarg checks;
  new test verifies history is populated before stale-confirmation call

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: negative feedback system for false positive reduction

Add rejected_matches table to record "this face is NOT this person"
decisions. When a user clears an auto-match, the rejection pair is saved
and excluded from future match_face() and find_person_in_photos() calls.

Key design decisions:
- Keyed by (face_id, person_id) for per-face precision — rejecting
  person X on face A does not affect face B in the same photo
- No FK on face_id so rows survive delete_faces_for_photo (rescan)
- Rejection cleanup in db.insert_anchor() so all anchor paths
  (UI, MCP, photo_cards) clear stale rejections consistently
- Global rematch uses reset_auto_matches=True to recompute all
- UI refreshes open photo after global rematch

Also: global rematch button on dashboard, face_id labels on canvas,
rejection indicator in expanded face cards, rejection count in dashboard.

Add calendar integration (Google/Outlook), meeting edit/cancel/reschedule

Backend:

- db.py: OAuth token storage, state nonce CSRF, activity log, cancel/edit/reschedule
- main.py: 10+ new /health proxy actions (oauth_url, oauth_callback, calendar_status,
  calendar_disconnect, cancel, edit, reschedule, meeting_log); participant name
  enrichment; calendar write-back on booking
- models.py: MeetingEditSchema, MeetingLogEntry, ConnectedCalendar
- terraform/dynamodb.tf: enable TTL on ttlExpiry attribute
- terraform/lambda.tf: FRONTEND_URL env var + OAuth credential placeholders

Frontend:
- apiClient.js: routes for cancel, edit, reschedule, meeting_log, calendar_status,
  oauth_url, oauth_callback, calendar_disconnect
- MeetingDashboard.jsx: edit modal, cancel confirmation, reschedule confirmation,
  cancelled meetings section, participant name display

feat(data): add addr layer (house numbers) to PMTiles (#111)

- config.json: new 'addr' layer at minzoom 14, maxzoom 15
- process.lua: extract addr:housenumber nodes to addr layer
- process.lua: extract building way centroids with addr:housenumber to addr layer
- Both node and way addresses include street attribute when available
- Added 'addr:housenumber' to node_keys for tilemaker filtering

Closes #103

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Gazebo GUI display and point cloud recording issues

- Switch GUI from XQuartz X11 to Xvfb+VNC (x11vnc on port 5900)
  XQuartz causes gzclient SIGSEGV on Apple Silicon; VNC via TigerVNC works reliably
- Add start_sim.sh: starts Xvfb virtual display, x11vnc, then ROS2 sim
- docker-compose.yml: use DISPLAY=:99 + llvmpipe software rendering, expose port 5900
- Dockerfile: add x11vnc package

- Fix 3D LiDAR producing empty PointCloud2 in MCAP recordings
  CPU ray sensor (type="ray") ignores vertical scan config; switch to gpu_ray
  which uses OpenGL FBO rendering and properly generates multi-layer point clouds

- Fix spurious "Could not find the 'robot' element" gzserver error with record:=true
  ros2 bag record triggers latched /robot_description republish, causing Gazebo
  to re-parse the URDF and fail; fix by spawning from a temp file (-file) instead
  of from the topic (-topic) so Gazebo only parses the model once

- Update all docs: README, RUNNING_LOCAL.md, src/robot_control/README.md

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Визуальное выделение изменений: зелёная рамка + лейблы НОВОЕ/ОБНОВЛЕНО

Добавлены CSS-класс .updated и кнопка-переключатель для 8 блоков:
- 5 регуляторных трендов СОЦПРОФ (НОВОЕ)
- 13 конкурентов расширенного ландшафта (НОВОЕ)
- Расшифровка «Прочие 18%» (НОВОЕ)
- Методология ценообразования (НОВОЕ)
- Donut-chart долей рынка (ОБНОВЛЕНО — 1С:ПБ→EHS)
- 1С:EHS карточка конкурента (ОБНОВЛЕНО)
- Тепловая карта модулей (ОБНОВЛЕНО — 12/14)
- Продуктовый анализ КОТ (ОБНОВЛЕНО — без экомониторинга)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix fhirpath R4 model import for ESM compatibility

Replace require() with ESM default import for fhirpath/fhir-context/r4.
Webpack was converting require() to a namespace import (import * as),
which wraps CJS modules in { default: ... } instead of providing the
model directly to fhirpath.compile().

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix story narrative TTS timeout: cap at 3 sentences / 80 words

GPT-4o was generating ~200 words despite instructions, causing AWS Polly
TTS to take 38s+ and triggering WebSocket keepalive timeout. Reduced
prompt to 3 sentences max (40-60 words), lowered max_tokens 300→150,
and added hard 80-word truncation safety net.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
